title: Sweet Triangle Treats Take Over October
story: Those tiny orange, yellow, and white triangles are taking over candy bowls everywhere because October 30 is National Candy Corn Day! People celebrate by munching on millions of these waxy sweet treats that taste like pure sugar magic. Some folks love them, others think they're weird, but everyone has an opinion about candy corn. You'll see them piled high in stores, mixed into Halloween trail mix, and used as decorations. Creative candy lovers even use them for art projects or sort them by color before eating. Whether you're team candy corn or not, this polarizing treat gets its special day right before Halloween!
activity_question: What colors make up a piece of candy corn?
bonus_fact: Americans buy over 35 million pounds of candy corn each year!

title: Check It Out: National Checklist Day
story: People everywhere grab their pens and make lists on October 30 for National Checklist Day! This fun holiday celebrates the simple joy of writing down tasks and checking them off. Kids make homework lists, parents organize chores, and even teachers create classroom activities. The best part? That satisfying feeling when you draw a big checkmark next to something you finished!
activity_question: What do people enjoy most about making checklists on this holiday?
activity_choices: The satisfying feeling of checking things off
activity_answer: The satisfying feeling of checking things off
follow_up_question: What would be on your perfect checklist for a fun day?

title: Mischief Night: Pranks Before Halloween
story: The night before Halloween gets its own special celebration called Mischief Night on October 30! Kids and teens pull harmless pranks like toilet papering trees, soaping windows, or ringing doorbells and running away. Some neighborhoods see silly tricks like moving lawn decorations or leaving fake spiders on doorsteps. Parents often join the fun by helping plan safe pranks or setting up spooky surprises for trick-or-treaters the next day. It's like a practice round for Halloween mischief!
activity_question: What is a common Mischief Night prank mentioned in the story?
follow_up_question: What harmless prank would you want to try on Mischief Night?

title: Devil's Night: Spooky Fun Before Halloween
story: The night before Halloween gets extra spooky on October 30 with Devil's Night! This tradition started in Detroit but now happens in cities across America. Kids and teens pull harmless pranks like toilet papering trees, soaping windows, or ringing doorbells and running away. Some neighborhoods organize safe Devil's Night events with costume contests and ghost stories. Police often patrol more on this night to keep everyone safe. It's like a practice round for Halloween - all the mischief without the candy!
activity_question: What city is famous for starting the Devil's Night tradition?
follow_up_question: What harmless prank would you want to try on Devil's Night?
